# Arrow Resilience Kit - Documentation

This directory contains the auto-generated API documentation and additional guides.

## 📚 API Documentation (Auto-Generated)

The root of this directory contains the **Dokka-generated API documentation** which is automatically built and deployed to GitHub Pages.

**View Online**: https://sorinirimies.github.io/arrow-resilience-kit/

### Auto-Generation

The API documentation is automatically generated and committed to this directory by GitHub Actions workflows:

- **On Release**: When a new GitHub Release is created (`.github/workflows/publish.yml`)
- **On Code Changes**: When source code is pushed to main (`.github/workflows/docs.yml`)

### Structure

After generation, this directory contains:
```
docs/
├── index.html                      # API documentation entry point
├── navigation.html                 # Navigation menu
├── arrow-resilience-kit/          # Module documentation
│   └── ro.sorinirimies.arrow.resiliencekit/
│       ├── index.html              # Package overview
│       ├── -circuit-breaker/      # CircuitBreaker docs
│       ├── -rate-limiter/          # RateLimiter docs
│       ├── -bulkhead/              # Bulkhead docs
│       └── ...                     # Other classes
├── scripts/                        # JavaScript files
├── styles/                         # Stylesheets
├── .nojekyll                       # Disables Jekyll processing
├── BUILD_INFO.html                 # Build metadata
├── VERSION.html                    # Version info (releases)
└── guides/                         # 👉 Documentation guides (see below)
```

## 📖 Documentation Guides

The `guides/` subdirectory contains **hand-written documentation guides** that are NOT auto-generated:

### For Developers

- **[DOCUMENTATION_SETUP.md](guides/DOCUMENTATION_SETUP.md)** - How to generate and deploy API documentation
- **[GIT_CLIFF_GUIDE.md](guides/GIT_CLIFF_GUIDE.md)** - How to use git-cliff for changelog generation

### For Maintainers

- **[PUBLISHING.md](guides/PUBLISHING.md)** - Publishing and release documentation

## 🔄 Important Notes

### Do NOT Manually Edit

The root files in `docs/` (HTML, CSS, JS) are **auto-generated by Dokka** and will be overwritten. Do not manually edit them.

### Keeping Guides Safe

The `guides/` subdirectory is **protected** - it contains hand-written documentation that should be manually maintained and is not overwritten by the build process.

### Local Development

To generate documentation locally:

```bash
# Generate Dokka documentation
./gradlew dokkaHtml

# Output will be in: build/docs/

# Prepare for GitHub Pages (copies to docs/)
./gradlew prepareDocs
```

### GitHub Pages Configuration

This directory is served by GitHub Pages from the `main` branch:

- **Repository Settings** → **Pages** → **Source**: Deploy from a branch
- **Branch**: `main`
- **Folder**: `/docs`

## 📝 Adding New Guides

To add a new documentation guide:

1. Create the file in `docs/guides/`
2. Link to it from the main [README.md](../README.md)
3. Commit and push

Example:
```bash
# Create a new guide
vi docs/guides/ARCHITECTURE.md

# Link from main README
# Add to "Documentation" section

# Commit
git add docs/guides/ARCHITECTURE.md README.md
git commit -m "docs: add architecture guide"
git push
```

## ⚠️ .gitignore

**Note**: The `docs/` directory is **NOT in .gitignore** because GitHub Pages needs these files to be committed to the repository.

The auto-generated files are managed by CI/CD workflows and should be committed as part of the release process.
